parts.title: SKILL 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS
parts.instruction: Negative expressions are very common in the short dialogues, and the most common kind of correct response to a negative statement is a positive statement containing a word with an opposite meaning.

questions.passage:
(man) Are you going to take out the trash?
(woman) I have no time to do it.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She is very busy.
She has lots of free time.
It is not necessary to take out the trash.
She will do it if she has time.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you worried about the interview?
(man) It's unimportant to me.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The interview is very important.
He is worried about the interview.
What he's wearing to the interview is important.
He is not concerned about the interview.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you have all the notes for Psychol ogy 101?
(man) I didn't miss a single lecture.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He has almost all the notes.
His attendance was perfect.
He went to all the lectures but one.
He missed more than one psychology class.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you know the library's summer hours? I need to go there this evening.
(man) It's never open past six o'clock in the summer.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: They passed the library at 6:00.
The library opens at 6:00 in the summer.
The library closes at 6:00.
You can't check out more than six books in the summer.

questions.passage:
(woman) How often should I water the plants while you're gone?
(man) No more than once a week.
questions.question: 5. WHAT SHOULD THE WOMAN DO?

options.text: Water the plants once a day.
Give the plants no more water.
Water the plants often while the man is gone.
Give the plants a limited amount of water.

questions.passage:
(man) Did you enjoy your meal? That restau rant is very famous.
(woman) The food was good, but I was dissatis fied with the service.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The service satisfied her.
The food was worse than the service.
She thought the service was bad.
Neither the food nor the service was satisfying.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think that Bob really wanted me to go home?
(man) He wasn't kidding when he told you to leave.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT BOB?

options.text: He told his kids to leave.
He seriously wanted the woman to go.
He was joking when he told the woman to leave.
He left with the woman.

questions.passage:
(woman) Can we finish this project before clos ing time?
(man) If we work on nothing else.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The project will take all their effort.
They have no other work to do.
It's impossible to finish.
They aren't even close to finishing the project.

questions.passage:
(man) Would you mind staying an hour more?
(woman) I'd rather not.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT STAYING?

options.text: She doesn't mind an hour more.
She'd rather stay more than an hour.
It's better to stay than go.
She prefers to leave.

questions.passage:
(woman) Would you recommend the hotel where you stayed in New York?
(man) The hotel provided service that was unequaled!
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT THE HOTEL?

options.text: The service at the hotel wasn't too good.
This hotel gave excellent service.
The service at the hotel could have been improved.
This hotel's service was the same as the service at other hotels.

parts.title: SKILL 8: LISTEN FOR DOUBLE NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS

questions.passage:
(man) Do you think Ron Rogers will be elected?
(woman) Well, it's not completely impossible.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY ABOUT RON?

options.text: He'll definitely be elected.
The election is now complete.
She has high hopes for his chances.
It may happen.

questions.passage:
(woman) How was your tennis match today?
(man) I didn't serve well, and I didn't volley well either.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT HIS TENNIS GAME?

options.text: Both parts of his game were bad.
He served better than he volleyed.
Some parts of his game were better than others.
He played rather well.

questions.passage:
(man) Was Gary prepared for the debate?
(woman) It is no surprise that he was unpre pared.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT GARY?

options.text: It is a surprise that he was prepared.
He was not ready, as usual.
He prepared a really big surprise.
His strong preparation came as no surprise.

questions.passage:
(man) Did you go out dancing with everyone else last night?
(woman) I was not feeling well, so I didn't go out.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She felt good enough to go out.
She went out to get some medicine.
She felt like dancing, so she went out with everyone.
She stayed home because she was sick.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think Paula understands what she's done?
(man) She isn't unaware of the trouble she's caused.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT PAULA?

options.text: She has problems that others aren't aware of.
Others aren't aware of her problems.
She knows she's been a problem.
She doesn't have a care in the world.

questions.passage:
(man) Did your friends finish the term paper for history class?
(woman) Steve wasn't able to finish it, and Paul wasn't either.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Steve wanted to finish his paper, and so did Paul.
Both Steve's and Paul's papers were incomplete.
Steve and Paul were busy doing their term papers.
When Steve wasn't able to finish his paper, Paul couldn't help.

questions.passage:
(man) Can you believe that George walked out of the restaurant without paying for his share of the meal?
(woman) It was irresponsible of him not to pay the bill.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: It wasn't George's responsibility to pay the bill.
Bill was irresponsible about paying George's rent.
George acted carelessly by not taking care of the bill.
George took responsibility for the unpaid bill.

questions.passage:
(man) What happened when Harry applied to Milhouse University?
(woman) It was unfortunate that he wasn't admitted to the university.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT HARRY?

options.text: It's fortunate that he was accepted.
It's good that he wasn't admitted.
Fortunately, the university didn't admit him.
It's too bad he was rejected.

questions.passage:
(woman) What did you think of the essay that I wrote?
(man) The first draft of the essay wasn't well written, and the second wasn't much better.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The first essay was better than the second.
The first and second drafts couldn't be better.
The second draft of the essay was much better than the first.
Both versions were poorly written.

questions.passage:
(man) Has Roger been disturbed by all of the recent problems?
(woman) He hasn't been unaffected.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Roger has been bothered.
Roger wasn't the least bit disturbed.
The problems have had little effect on Roger.
Roger hasn't been disturbed.

parts.title: SKILL 9: LISTEN FOR "ALMOST NEGATIVE" EXPRESSIONS
parts.instruction: Certain expressions in English have "almost negative" meanings. These expressions are common in the short dialogues.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you expect a lot of rain this month?
(man) It hardly ever rains in July.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: There's little rain in July.
In July it never rains.
It rains hard in July.
When it rains in July, it rains hard.

questions.passage:
(man) Were all three students accepted to the university?
(woman) Only John was accepted.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The university accepted three students.
None of the students is going to the university.
John was not accepted.
Two were not admitted.

questions.passage:
(man) Did Mark do well in Professor
(woman) Franks's class?
questions.question: 3. Mark barely passed the history exam that Professor Franks gave. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Although he did pass, Mark's exam grade wasn't too good.
Mark failed his history exam.
The highest grade on the history exam went to Mark.
Professor Franks didn't pass Mark on the history exam.

questions.passage:
(woman) I can't believe how long we've been here.
(man) Dr. Roberts almost never keeps his patients waiting long.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He often has long waits in Dr.
Roberts's office.
He must wait patiently for Robert.
Dr. Roberts is generally punctual.

questions.passage:
(man) I can't believe Betty is not at work this week!
(woman) Only rarely does Betty take a vacation in winter.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: He doesn't mind waiting for Dr.
Roberts.
Betty often takes vacations in winter.
Betty prefers to take vacations in winter.

questions.passage:
(man) Does Steve study very much?
(woman) He hardly ever opens a book.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT STEVE?

options.text: Occasionally Betty works one week during vacation.
A winter vacation is unusual for Betty.
He rarely spends time on his courses.
He's an excellent student.

questions.passage:
(woman) Was the philosophy exam very long?
(man) I scarcely had time to finish it.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He never studies.
His books are always open.
He finished the exam in plenty of time.
He was scared he wouldn't finish.

questions.passage:
(man) I was so bored at the staff meeting!
(woman) Seldom have staff meetings lasted this long.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: He used every possible minute to finish.
He was unable to complete the exam.
This was a very long staff meeting.
This was the only staff meeting in a long time.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you enjoying the barbecue?
((man)  Only rarely have I tasted such deli cious meat.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The meeting lasted only until one o'clock.
The one staff meeting should've lasted longer.
Meat tastes delicious to him when it's cooked rare.
He isn't sure if the meal is delicious.

questions.passage:
(woman) Does your broken arm hurt very much?
(man) Only if I try to move.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: This meat is the best he's tasted in a long time.
He'd like to eat some meat from this delicatessen.
He broke his arm trying to move it.
He only hurt the broken arm.

parts.title: SKILL 10: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVES WITH COMPARATIVES
parts.instruction: Negatives can be used with comparatives in the short dialogues of the TOEFL test. A sentence with a negative and a comparative has a superlative, or very strong, meaning.

questions.passage:
(man) Are you pleased with the exam results?
(woman) I couldn't be happier.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's not very happy.
She didn't do very well on the exam.
She could be somewhat happier.
She's delighted with the results.

questions.passage:
(woman) Is Paula lazy, as usual, this semester?
(man) She's never tried harder.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Paula is always lazy.
Paula didn't work very hard this semester.
Paula made a strong effort.
Paula could have worked harder.

questions.passage:
(man) Was it a good sale? Did you buy a lot?
(woman) Prices couldn't have been any lower.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE SALE?

options.text: The prices were great! .
The prices were too high.
She didn't buy much because of the prices.
The prices could have been lower.

questions.passage:
(woman) What do you think of Betsy?
(man) I don't know a more intelligent woman.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT BETSY?

options.text: She is not very smart.
She always tells him everything.
He doesn't know her very well.
She's extremely intelligent.

questions.passage:
(woman) Did the patient really need the surgery?
(man) It couldn't have been more unneces sary.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The patient absolutely didn't need the surgery.
The necessity for the surgery was unquestionable.
The surgeon felt that the operation was necessary.
It was essential that the surgery be performed immediately.

questions.passage:
(man) Did any of you get hurt in the acci dent?
(woman) We couldn't have been luckier.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: They were not very lucky.
No one was hurt.
The accident was unfortunate.
She wanted to have better luck.

questions.passage:
(woman) How do you think you did on the final exam in biology?
(man) Nothing could have been more diffi cult than that exam!
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Nothing was very difficult.
The exam wasn't at all easy.
The exam couldn't have been easier.
The exam had nothing difficult on it.

questions.passage:
(man) Are you ready to accept the new posi tion?
(woman) Nobody wants that job more than I do.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She wants that job very much.
No one is going to get the job.
Everybody else wants that job as much as she does.
She is not sure about taking the job.

questions.passage:
(man) How did you do in the race?
(woman) Only one person was faster.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She was second in the race.
She was almost the slowest person in the race.
She won the race.
She was not faster than anyone else.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think that the math project this semester was easy?
(man) Seldom has a math project been more complicated.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: This math project was extremely complex.
This math project was less complicated than the last.
They seldom complete their math projects.
Complicated math projects are often assigned.

questions.passage:
(man) Do you think you could try a little harder?
(woman) It's impossible for me to do more.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She can try a little harder.
There is a lot more that she can do.
She's doing the best that she can.
It is impossible for her to do anything.

questions.passage:
(man) What are you so upset about?
(woman) Not once on this trip has the bus left on time.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's always been late for the bus.
The bus has always been late.
The bus only left on time once.
Only on this trip has the bus been on time.

questions.passage:
(man) Was there enough soup to go around?
(woman) There was barely enough soup for everyone at the table.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: There wasn't enough soup to go around.
We had so much soup that we couldn't finish it.
Everyone got one serving of soup, but there wasn't enough for seconds.
Everyone around the table had a lot of soup.

questions.passage:
(man) Do you really want to move to Florida?
(woman) I couldn't want anything more!
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She does want to see the movie.
It's extremely important to her to go.
She doesn't want to go there anymore.
She really couldn't move there.

questions.passage:
(man) Did you turn in your research paper for history class?
(woman) I couldn't turn it in because it was incomplete.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She handed the paper in on time.
She was able to complete the paper, but she didn't turn it in.
The paper was a complete mess, so she didn't turn it in.
The paper was unfinished.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think that your friends enjoyed their trip to the Museum of
(man) Modern Art?
questions.question: 6. Sylvia doesn't care for modern art, and neither does Tim. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Neither Tim nor Sylvia is taking care of Art.
Sylvia likes modern art even less than Tim does.
Sylvia doesn't care for anything Tim does.
Sylvia and Tim agree in their opinion of modern art.

questions.passage:
(woman) Can your friends go with us this after noon, or do you think they will be too busy?
(man) They hardly ever work in the after noon.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT HIS FRIENDS?

options.text: They always work hard in the afternoon.
They don't do much after lunch.
After noon they never work.
It's never hard for them to work in the afternoon.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think we could turn the air conditioner on? I'm really uncomfort able?
(man) The air-conditioner never works when it gets warm.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: It's hard for him to work when it gets warm.
Whenever it gets warm, he turns on the air-conditioner.
The air-conditioner only works when it isn't needed.
He likes to use the air-conditioner when it is warm.

questions.passage:
(woman) I heard that your exam results were not too bad.
(man) Actually, I've never done worse.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He did really poorly.
He's felt worse before.
The results co\ild not have been better.
He's not too unhappy with the results.

questions.passage:
(man) Did the committee come to a decision about the parking problem?
(woman) The committee shouldn't have decided the issue when so many mem bers weren't present.
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: With so many members present, the committee couldn't reach a decision.
The committee should've waited until more members were present.
The issue shouldn't have been decided by all the committed members.
The issue wasn't decided because so many members were absent.

questions.passage:
(man)         What should I do to get over this?
(woman)       You should drink plenty of fluids, take this medicine once every eight hours, and return here to my office next week.
questions.question: 1.       WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSA TION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: In a doctor's office .
At a bar .
In a travel agency .
In a business office .

questions.passage:
(man)         Did you find anything at the store?
(woman)       I just bought a great shirt!
questions.question: 2.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She bought some sheets.
She got a new piece of clothing.
She couldn't find anything because she's too short.
She was sure to greet her boss.

questions.passage:
(man)         Did you enjoy the hotel where you stayed in Hawaii?
(woman)       The view of the ocean couldn't have been better.
questions.question: 3.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The hotel was all right, except for the poor view.
The view from the hotel room was spectacular.
She would have preferred a better hotel.
Only a few hotels would have been better.

questions.passage:
(woman)       You look like you're not feeling too well.
(man)         Actually, I'm just kind of tired. All I need is a bit of rest.
questions.question: 4.       WHAT WILL THE MAN PROBABLY DO NEXT?

options.text: Take a nap .
Try the rest of the work .
See a doctor .
Have a bite to eat .

questions.passage:
(woman)       Do you think that Mary will forgive me for what I did?
(man)         She isn't exactly an unforgiving per son.
questions.question: 5.       WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT MARY?

options.text: She's an exacting person.
She can't be expected to give you four of them.
She generally forgives others.
She isn't exact about what she gives to others.

questions.passage:
(man)         Has Martha already gone on vacation?
(woman)       She can't take her vacation until next week.
questions.question: 6.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT MARTHA?

options.text: She's unable to take her vacation this year.
Her vacation next week has been postponed.
She'll go on vacation next week.
She'll return from vacation in a week.

questions.passage:
(man)         I'm looking for some people who just came into the restaurant.
(woman)       The waitress seated them at a table in the back.
questions.question: 7.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The waitress was sitting in the back of the restaurant.
They were waiting for a seat in the restaurant.
The customers had a table in the back.
The waitress sat down behind the table.

questions.passage:
(man)         Let's go to the market and get some fresh fruit.
(woman)       I was there this morning, and the mar ket scarcely had any.
questions.question: 8.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE MARKET?

options.text: It's hard for the market to sell its fruit.
All of the fresh fruit at the market is hard.
She hardly ever goes to the market to buy fresh fruit.
There was a scarcity of fresh fruit at the market.

questions.passage:
(man)         I'm thinking about taking a few classes at the local adult school.
(woman)       It's never too late to go back to school.
questions.question: 9.       WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The man should never be late for school.
The man can always return to school.
The man should never go back to school.
If the man's late to school, he should go through the back door.

questions.passage:
(man)         I don't think I can make it all the way to the top of the mountain.
(woman)       I dare you to try!
questions.question: 10.      WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She can't bear to try.
She is a daring person.
She doesn't want the man even to try.
She is challenging the man to make the effort.
